candidates.label: Short celebratory statements
candidates.description: Brief, single-sentence holiday or event greetings in informal tone.

citations.text: Happy National Sons day Zachary Spartacus Cooke and the rest.

candidates.scope_note: Observations are limited to posts in this batch.
candidates.boundary_note: Not all brief statements are celebratory; for example, "New boots y’all!" is informal but not a holiday greeting.

candidates.label: Structured daily challenge posts
candidates.description: Posts that include a day number and an explicit challenge or task.

citations.text: Day 1: I’ve been challenged to share my life for 7 days, no people, no words, just pictures, (unless I'm asked a question). Anne Elliott Payne
Day 2: I’ve been challenged to share my life for 7 days, no people, no words, just pictures, (unless I'm asked a question). Anne Elliott Payne
Day #2 Today I challenge Dixie Normous
Day 3. Sorry Dude. Today I challenge Marvin Gardens.

candidates.scope_note: Observations are limited to posts in this batch.
candidates.boundary_note: Some posts use the "Day" format for other purposes that do not involve a challenge; these would need separate classification.

candidates.label: Pun/wordplay in casual posts
candidates.description: Informal posts containing playful word substitutions or puns.

citations.text: All these Easter puns are getting ridiculous. You peeps aren’t even bunny.

candidates.scope_note: Observations are limited to posts in this batch.
candidates.boundary_note: Not all informal statements include puns; for example, "Happy siblings day or whatever." is playful but not a pun.
